title: add e2e-bot Keycloak user provisioning script
description:
## Summary
Adds `scripts/e2e-bot-setup.sh` — an idempotent provisioner for a dedicated `e2e-bot` workspace user that the Playwright suite (sa-08-sso, OIDC flows) logs in as.

## Why
`E2E (Live Production)` (PR #518) needs `MM_TEST_USER` / `MM_TEST_PASS` to drive auth-gated specs. We didn't want to upload personal admin creds to GH Actions secrets on a public repo, so this provisions a separate test-only user.

No realm-admin role — auth-gated specs only need a logged-in workspace user, and most `fa-admin-*` specs assert the unauthenticated path anyway. Easy to grant admin later if needed.

## What's in the script
- Reads `KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD` from `workspace-secrets` in the right namespace via `kubectl --context $ENV_CONTEXT`
- Port-forwards Keycloak (port `18081` by default; override with `KC_LOCAL_PORT=`)
- Upserts user `e2e-bot` with email `e2e-bot@${PROD_DOMAIN}`
- Generates a policy-compliant random password if `E2E_BOT_PASSWORD` is unset; prints it on stdout
- Uses `--data-urlencode` for token requests so admin passwords containing `&`, `#`, `+`, etc. survive form encoding (a footgun the existing `admin-users-setup.sh` has but never tripped because mentolder's admin password lacks ampersands)

## What I ran already
Both clusters now have the user:
```
ENV=mentolder  E2E_BOT_PASSWORD=… bash scripts/e2e-bot-setup.sh
ENV=korczewski E2E_BOT_PASSWORD=… bash scripts/e2e-bot-setup.sh
```
Same password on both, stored locally at `environments/.secrets/e2e-bot.yaml` (gitignored) and as `MM_TEST_USER` / `MM_TEST_PASS` GH Actions secrets.

## Test plan
- [ ] After merge, trigger the `E2E (Live Production)` workflow with `cluster=both` and watch `sa-08-sso` go green on both matrix entries.
- [ ] Re-run `ENV=mentolder bash scripts/e2e-bot-setup.sh` (no password env) → confirms idempotent password reset.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
